title: Listening - Part 3: An Outdoor Festival
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about an outdoor festival they attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker enjoyed most about the festival. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about an outdoor festival they attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what each speaker enjoyed most about the festival. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about attending an outdoor festival.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: Our town's arts festival runs all afternoon, but because I was working, I didn't arrive until early evening. I worried that I had missed the best activities. Actually, that timing made the visit special. [19]Once it became dark, strings of lights came on between the trees, small bands played beside lanterns, and everyone sat on the grass as the park took on a magical atmosphere.[/19] I heard there were good workshops earlier, but the peaceful evening mood is what would persuade me to return.||Speaker 2 Man: I expected the craft festival to consist mainly of stalls selling expensive handmade objects. There were certainly plenty of those, and my sister bought a beautiful bowl. I didn't want to spend much. [20]Then I discovered a tent where visitors could sit with a potter and make a simple clay cup themselves, which was much more enjoyable than just looking at finished pieces.[/20] Mine is slightly uneven and probably useless for drinking from, but I brought it home proudly because I'd actually learned something new.||Speaker 3 Woman: My friends took me to an international festival because they wanted to hear the music. The concerts were lively, but the queues near the stages were long, so I kept wandering towards the quieter part of the park. [21]There I found stalls serving dishes from many different countries, and I loved being able to share small portions of unfamiliar food with friends and decide which flavours we liked best.[/21] By the end, I had barely watched a full performance but had written down several dishes to try cooking at home.||Speaker 4 Man: I usually avoid big festivals because they create mountains of rubbish: plastic cups, food containers and unwanted leaflets. A friend persuaded me to attend a local music festival that claimed to work differently. [22]I was impressed that drinks came in reusable cups, water stations were provided, and volunteers clearly explained how every item of waste should be sorted for recycling or composting.[/22] The bands were good too, but it was reassuring to enjoy an event without feeling that the park would be left covered in rubbish afterwards.||Speaker 5 Woman: I grow a few herbs on my balcony, so when I saw there was a gardening festival, I went hoping to buy one or two plants. There were far more flowers than I could afford. [23]The highlight was speaking to a well-known garden designer after her talk; she looked at photographs of my tiny balcony and suggested plants that would survive there without needing hours of attention.[/23] I followed her advice, and for the first time my containers still look healthy several months later.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: Our town's arts festival runs all afternoon, but because I was working, I didn't arrive until early evening. I worried that I had missed the best activities. Actually, that timing made the visit special. Once it became dark, strings of lights came on between the trees, small bands played beside lanterns, and everyone sat on the grass as the park took on a magical atmosphere. I heard there were good workshops earlier, but the peaceful evening mood is what would persuade me to return.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I expected the craft festival to consist mainly of stalls selling expensive handmade objects. There were certainly plenty of those, and my sister bought a beautiful bowl. I didn't want to spend much. Then I discovered a tent where visitors could sit with a potter and make a simple clay cup themselves, which was much more enjoyable than just looking at finished pieces. Mine is slightly uneven and probably useless for drinking from, but I brought it home proudly because I'd actually learned something new.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My friends took me to an international festival because they wanted to hear the music. The concerts were lively, but the queues near the stages were long, so I kept wandering towards the quieter part of the park. There I found stalls serving dishes from many different countries, and I loved being able to share small portions of unfamiliar food with friends and decide which flavours we liked best. By the end, I had barely watched a full performance but had written down several dishes to try cooking at home.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I usually avoid big festivals because they create mountains of rubbish: plastic cups, food containers and unwanted leaflets. A friend persuaded me to attend a local music festival that claimed to work differently. I was impressed that drinks came in reusable cups, water stations were provided, and volunteers clearly explained how every item of waste should be sorted for recycling or composting. The bands were good too, but it was reassuring to enjoy an event without feeling that the park would be left covered in rubbish afterwards.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I grow a few herbs on my balcony, so when I saw there was a gardening festival, I went hoping to buy one or two plants. There were far more flowers than I could afford. The highlight was speaking to a well-known garden designer after her talk; she looked at photographs of my tiny balcony and suggested plants that would survive there without needing hours of attention. I followed her advice, and for the first time my containers still look healthy several months later.

options.text: the opportunity to try a skill
the chance to meet an expert

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker most appreciated the festival's atmosphere once the park was lit in the evening (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker enjoyed taking part in a practical pottery activity (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The variety of international dishes was the part of the festival the speaker enjoyed most (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker particularly admired the festival's measures to reduce and sort waste (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker valued getting personalised gardening advice from an expert (evidence [23]).
